title: Snake Bite
description: Immediate steps to take if bitten by a venomous snake.
steps: Keep the person incredibly calm and still. Movement can cause venom to spread faster.
Call an ambulance (108) immediately.
Remove tight clothing, rings, or watches near the bite area before swelling begins.
Wash the bite with soap and water if possible, but do not flush it aggressively.
Do NOT attempt to cut the wound or suck the venom out.
Do NOT apply a tourniquet. Instead, apply a loose, sterile bandage over the bite.
Keep the bitten limb positioned at or slightly below the level of the heart.

title: Fracture (Broken Bone)
description: How to handle suspected bone fractures to prevent further damage.
steps: Stop any bleeding by applying pressure with a sterile bandage.
Immobilize the injured area immediately. Do NOT try to realign the bone.
Apply ice packs to limit swelling and help relieve pain (do not apply ice directly to skin).
Treat for shock: lay the person flat, elevate legs if appropriate.
Seek emergency medical help quickly.

title: Burns
description: First aid for thermal or chemical burns.
steps: Remove the person from the heat source to stop the burning.
Cool the burn by holding it under cool (not ice-cold) running water for 10-15 minutes.
Remove rings or tight items from the burned area quickly and gently.
Cover the burn loosely with a sterile, non-fluffy bandage or clean cloth.
Do NOT break blisters. This introduces the risk of severe infection.
Seek medical help for major burns or burns larger than three inches.

title: Fainting
description: What to do if someone loses consciousness briefly.
steps: Catch the person as they fall if possible, and lay them flat on their back.
Elevate their legs 12 inches above heart level to restore blood flow to the brain.
Loosen restrictive collars, belts, and tight clothing.
Check their airway to ensure they are breathing normally.
If they don't regain consciousness within 1 minute, call for emergency medical assistance (108).
Do NOT splash water on their face or force them to drink.

title: Choking
description: How to clear a blocked airway using the Heimlich maneuver.
steps: Ask 'Are you choking?' If they cannot cough or speak, act immediately.
Stand behind them, wrap your arms around their waist, and lean them slightly forward.
Make a fist with one hand and place it just above their navel.
Grasp your fist with the other hand and give 5 quick, upward thrusts.
Repeat the abdominal thrusts until the object is expelled.
If the person becomes unconscious, lower them to the ground and begin CPR.
